title: POS-ISP: Pipeline Optimization at the Sequence Level for Task-aware ISP
problem: Optimizing Image Signal Processor (ISP) pipelines for computer vision tasks like object detection and semantic segmentation is challenging due to time-consuming manual tuning and limitations of existing automated approaches.

existingApproaches.name: Modular approaches
existingApproaches.description: Decompose ISP pipeline into operations like white balance and denoising, optimized in a task-driven manner.
existingApproaches.limitations: Optimizing remains difficult due to non-differentiable search procedures for selecting modules and tuning parameters.

existingApproaches.name: Neural Architecture Search (NAS)
existingApproaches.description: Enables gradient-based optimization by mixing outputs of candidate modules.
existingApproaches.limitations: Reliance on mixture training causes inconsistency at inference where modules are discretely selected.

existingApproaches.name: Reinforcement Learning (RL)
existingApproaches.description: Models ISP optimization as a stepwise RL formulation with sequential decision-making at each intermediate stage.
existingApproaches.limitations: Requires repeated evaluations and relies on future reward estimation, resulting in unstable training and high computational overhead.

proposedSolution.name: POS-ISP
proposedSolution.approach: Sequence-level optimization by evaluating the entire pipeline with a single final reward

components.name: sequence predictor
components.description: A recurrent policy network that predicts the entire module sequence by leveraging contextual information from preceding modules

advantages: Suitable for deployment on mobile or edge devices
